subject: Human Resources
title: Feedback Culture
description: A short lesson to help you apply Feedback Culture.

hook.text: An employee hasn't received feedback in eight months. They assume everything's fine—until a surprise poor review.

buildup.text: Feedback culture means regular, two-way conversations about performance—not just annual reviews from the top down.

discovery.text: Continuous feedback improves performance by 12%. Employees crave it—65% say they want more feedback than they get.

twist.text: Feedback must go both ways. Leaders who accept feedback from their teams create cultures where honest dialogue thrives.

climax.text: The best feedback is specific, timely, and focused on behavior—not personality. It helps people grow, not shrink.

punchline.text: Feedback is a gift—but only when it's given often enough to matter.

quiz.question: What do 65% of employees say about feedback?
options: They want more than they currently receive
